title: Aporum teres
taxonomy: Aporum teres is classified under the family Orchidaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. This family includes more than 30,000 known species, and Aporum teres is one of the many species within it. Aporum teres is further classified as a genus within the subfamily Epidendroideae. The subfamily Epidendroideae is a large group of orchids that are mostly epiphytes, meaning they grow on other plants for support. intro: Aporum teres, also known as the "Sarangkot orchid," is a species of flowering orchid native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Aporum teres include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Aporum teres is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
